unit.note: F0 full suite GREEN — 18 units, 1.7 min.

e2e.note: no runtime surface: a baseline JSON entry and a documentation line

degraded.condition: Group H2 still reports one entry
degraded.reason: shared/scripts/tools/verifiers/common.py is held at its recorded 771 instead of being tightened to 766. The in-flight iterate events-record-boundary-readers takes that file to 768, so tightening it here would block that PR on the anti-ratchet. Deliberate; it tightens once that work lands.

degraded.condition: Compliance D/D1 and D/D3 (FR-01.15) are NOT addressed
degraded.reason: Both say FR-01.15 was promised via new_frs and never reaffirmed by a later event. The only way to clear them from this iterate would be to declare affected_frs: [FR-01.15] — which would be FALSE, because this change does not touch the cross-repo output contract. The findings are telling the truth and must clear when real work touches that requirement, not by an accurate-looking declaration.

degraded.condition: complexity declared small, classifier said medium
degraded.reason: The classifier's own scope signal is 'trivial'; 'medium' comes from the history prior, not from this change. Two files, no code path, no behaviour. Recorded because a downgrade must never be silent — note that it dodges no gate here: the cross-layer gate has nothing to evaluate on a diff with no FR or spec delta.

surface_verification.justification: No runtime surface. The diff is one entry set in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json plus one bullet in conventions.md. Verification is the Group H audit itself plus the two meta-tests that consume these artifacts.

behaviors.behavior: Four files that drifted over the 300-line limit are registered in the baseline, so Group H1 passes and the anti-ratchet is armed on them
behaviors.evidence: group_h.run() → H1 pass: 'all 156 oversize file(s) are listed in baseline'

behaviors.behavior: Nine baseline entries whose recorded size was looser than the file are tightened to the real count (ratchet down only, never up)
behaviors.evidence: group_h.run() → H2 residual is 1 entry, deliberately held (see below)

behaviors.behavior: The one entry an in-flight iterate would grow past is NOT tightened
behaviors.evidence: measured the events-record-boundary worktree: common.py is 768 there vs the 766 a tightening would pin — held at 771. group_d shrinks to 449, so tightening it to 464 is safe and was applied.

behaviors.behavior: REQ-0's arch-impact drop has its obliged conventions.md bullet, so the routing check passes
The new conventions.md entry is inside the one-line budget

behaviors.evidence: F0 full suite GREEN, 18 units, 1.7 min; ruff clean; diff is one JSON baseline + one documentation line
